title: Telex Application Client (supported long-lived application integration)
summary: Builder-approved contract-convergence node #118 is active in exact-plan review; ADR 0049 is reserved, application-client-ready remains pending, and all later nodes are planned but blocked on that semantic gate.

nodes.title: Converge shared Application Client contract
nodes.summary: Active planning for issue #118: obtain fresh dual exact approval for planning-reviewed revision 14 at commit 626a80a, then reconcile every merged Watcher and Operator requirement through issue #12. ADR 0049 is allocated but not landed; no contract or publication work begins before the plan gate.

nodes.title: Shared semantic contract accepted
nodes.summary: Pending semantic gate: campaign validates the merged contract, per-requirement dispositions, consumer approvals, merged-source provenance, ADR 0049, and exact issue #12 publication before exporting application-client-ready to both product workstreams.

nodes.title: Supported Application Client core
nodes.summary: Implement the accepted semantic contract as one supported application client core over existing Telex daemon/backends, including lifecycle, capability, send/receive, operations, identity, health, recovery, and backend selection. Sketch; detail after application-client-ready.

nodes.title: First supported language binding
nodes.summary: Expose the accepted client core through the first binding required by Operator Station and SDK-hosted applications while preserving semantic parity and avoiding binding-specific forks. Sketch; detail after the core export is accepted.

nodes.title: Application Client conformance and migration
nodes.summary: Prove the supported core and binding against SQLite/Postgres, restart and membership loss, multi-address compensation, send-only/bidirectional capability, delivery-row identity, receipt-state separation, retry-safe operations, ordered resync, and migration away from temporary seams. Sketch; detail after implementation shape is known.

nodes.title: Supported client is consumable by both product workstreams
nodes.summary: Operator Station and Watcher validate the supported client against their merged contracts and confirm no product-private integration seam is required before product integration continues.

nodes.title: Application Client operational hardening
nodes.summary: Harden packaging, install/upgrade, diagnostics, cleanup, resource bounds, credentialed Postgres, delayed or duplicated delivery, process replacement, and additional binding ergonomics after both consumers can integrate. Sketch; detail after the consumer gate.

nodes.title: Application Client workstream reconciled
nodes.summary: Builder accepts the supported client, conformance evidence, consumer integration, migration guidance, and operational hardening, then reconciles durable lessons into both product workstreams and campaign closure.

checkpoints.title: Shared Application Client semantic contract accepted
checkpoints.summary: Pending: issue #12 must contain the approved merged contract and explicit dispositions before Operator Station and Watcher may detail and coordinate production implementation without private seams.

checkpoints.title: Supported client core and binding consumable
checkpoints.summary: The accepted contract has a supported implementation, first binding, conformance evidence, and consumer validation.

checkpoints.title: Application Client operationally validated
checkpoints.summary: The shared client has survived operational hardening and its exports are reconciled into both product workstreams.
